summary: DeVault argues that the ThinkPad X200 from 2008 remains the best laptop ever made due to its full Linux support, repairability, and open firmware compatibility. While acknowledging its weak battery life and inability to handle 1080p video, he contends that every modern laptop alternative fails in fundamental ways. He surveys the 2020 laptop market and finds nothing acceptable: Dell's firmware is broken, System76 and Purism are overpriced, Pine64 requires nonfree blobs, and others are perpetually out of stock. The post is a frustrated call for the industry to produce a laptop at least as good as the 12-year-old one he's stuck with.
key_points: The ThinkPad X200 supports upstream Linux, FreeBSD, NetBSD, Minix, Haiku, and even Plan 9, with coreboot compatibility and 26 user-replaceable parts
Modern laptops have improved only in battery life and video decoding; in every other respect the market has regressed
Every 2020 laptop alternative has crippling flaws: bad firmware, nonfree blobs, inflated prices, poor availability, or hostile design
Repairability, open firmware, and upstream driver support are the metrics that actually matter for a good laptop
money_quotes: The best laptop ever made is the ThinkPad X200, and I have two of them.
In every other respect, the market is regressive, half-assed garbage.
It has since come to my attention that in 2020, every laptop still fucking sucks.
Even the ones people pretend to like have crippling, egregious flaws.
The Dell XPS series has a firmware so bad that its engineers should be strung up in the town square for building it - if yours works, it's because you were lucky.
The laptop market is in an atrocious state, folks.
Come on, let's get our shit together and try to make a laptop which is at least as good as the 12 year-old one I'm stuck with now.
key_insight: The laptop industry has regressed on the things that actually matter — repairability, open firmware, and upstream driver support — while improving only on superficial specs like battery life and video performance.
